title: list: concurrent list.sort() of a shared list races (binarysort rewrites the detached array with no critical section)
description: list_sort_impl takes NO per-object critical section: it detaches the items array (ob_item=NULL, size=0) and, with no key func, sorts saved_ob_item (the list's own array) in place via binarysort. Two threads sorting the same shared list (or a concurrent reader holding the pre-detach ob_item) race on the array slots. RULED A BUG by Thomas Wouters (Yhg1s) 2026-07-15 (same shared-builtin class).
sites: Objects/listobject.c binarysort:1918 (a[L] = pivot -- rewrites the detached items array)
Objects/listobject.c list_sort_impl:2968-2977 (saves+detaches ob_item, no critical section; lo.keys = saved_ob_item)
race_kind: mutate-while-mutate on a shared builtin list (concurrent sort, no critical section)
shared_state: the shared list's items array (saved_ob_item, sorted in place)
severity: low observed (value-benign, crash-free), but an unsynchronized in-place rewrite of a shared list's array
repro: repro_attempt.py (concurrent sorters; does NOT reliably trigger in isolation)
repro_reliability: NOT reproduced in isolation -- the race window between list_sort_impl saving ob_item and nulling it is ~3 instructions; 4x200k and 16x400k concurrent-sort loops never hit it. The fleet caught it once.
suggested_fix: take the list's per-object critical section in list_sort_impl (or otherwise synchronize the detach+in-place-sort against concurrent readers/sorters), consistent with the other list mutators.
ruling: Thomas Wouters (Yhg1s, CPython RM), 2026-07-15: the shared-builtin concurrent-access class 'is a bug'; concurrent list.sort() falls under it.
notes: Formerly HELD as a dev-question ('is concurrent list.sort() on a shared list intended to stay crash-safe?'); the Yhg1s ruling answers it (yes, it's a bug). Root cause is grounded in the current-main list_sort_impl source; the isolated repro is the only missing piece. If filed, note the detach-without-lock mechanism explicitly.
